detection_signatures.notes: Snowdrop config files use a Ubisoft GSA_SDK XML wrapper. The root element is <GSA_SDK Version="1.0.x.x"> and settings are stored as <OPTION Name="..." Value="..."> nodes inside <GAMESETTINGS>. File names follow the pattern GFXSettings.<GameExeName>.exe.xml.

config_file_locations.notes: The CONFIGPATH attribute embedded in the XML file may point to a OneDrive-synced Documents folder (e.g. D:\OneDrive\Documents\...) rather than the canonical local path. On OneDrive-backed installs both paths resolve to the same physical file — edit either.

known_games: South Park: The Fractured But Whole

keys.notes: Present as an OPTION element in the GSA_SDK XML with values 'On' / 'Off'. Use G-Sync, FreeSync, or driver-level VSync instead of the in-game toggle. Verified in The Division; confirm the exact enum values in the game-specific profile for other titles.

keys.effect: Enables the DirectX 12 renderer instead of DirectX 11.
keys.notes: DX12 in Snowdrop titles has historically introduced stutter and inconsistent frame pacing on some hardware/driver combinations. DX11 is the safer default unless a game-specific profile explicitly recommends DX12. Verified in The Division; other Snowdrop titles may behave differently — always defer to the game-specific profile.

notes: Snowdrop is a Ubisoft proprietary engine. Unlike engines with a shared CVar namespace (e.g. Unreal Engine's sg.* / r.* prefixes), Snowdrop games each expose their own XML schema. There is no universal set of key names shared across all Snowdrop titles — always use a game-specific profile when one exists.
Config files are encoded in UTF-16 LE. Use Get-Content -Encoding Unicode (PowerShell) or an equivalent UTF-16 reader; treating the file as UTF-8 or ASCII will produce garbage output.
The GSA_SDK XML OPTION element lists valid enum values inline as child <ENUM EnumValue="..."> nodes. These can be inspected to confirm valid values for a given key without opening the game.
Snowdrop game config directories are typically located at %USERPROFILE%\Documents\My Games\<GameTitle>\.

sources.label: NVIDIA GeForce - Tom Clancy's The Division Graphics & Performance Guide
